steps.title: Identify what the student’s goal requires
steps.content:
The student wants to “specify when al-Biruni published his landmass theory.” The key word is when — the answer must state the publication date (1037 CE) from the notes. Any choice that omits the date fails the goal.
  • Choice A — 'In 1037 CE, al-Biruni published his theory that a large landmass existed west of Europe and east of Asia.': Opens with “In 1037 CE,” directly specifying when the theory was published. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Al-Biruni, who studied Earth’s physical features, published a theory about a large landmass.': Mentions publication but omits the year 1037 CE, so the “when” is never specified. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Al-Biruni was an Iranian scholar who studied Earth’s physical features.': Describes al-Biruni’s field of study; says nothing about his landmass theory or its publication date. ✗
  • Choice D — 'An Iranian scholar who studied Earth’s physical features, al-Biruni theorized that a large landmass existed west of Europe and east of Asia.': Describes the content of the theory but omits the publication year entirely. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the correct choice
steps.content: Only Choice A opens with the specific year the student needs to “specify when” the theory was published. That matches choice A — A is correct.
